pricing.faqBillingQ: How does billing work for 12 and 24-month plans?
pricing.faqBillingA: The full term is billed once at checkout at the discounted effective monthly rate. That is how we lock in your dedicated server for the whole contract. Included usage (voice minutes, SMS, AI budget) still resets every month.
pricing.faqTermEndQ: What happens when my term ends?
pricing.faqTermEndA: Service continues month-to-month at the renewal rate shown on your plan card (for example, {starterRenewal} for Starter or {standardRenewal} for Standard after a 24-month term), unless you start a new contract at the contract rate.
pricing.faqCarrierFeeQ: What is the one-time {carrierFee} carrier registration fee?
pricing.faqCarrierFeeA: US carriers require every business that sends text messages to register (10DLC brand and campaign registration). We pass this one-time {carrierFee} fee through at cost. It is charged once at signup and is non-refundable, since carriers do not refund it to us.
pricing.faqGuaranteeA: Yes. Every plan has a 30-day money-back window from the initial purchase date. The one-time carrier registration fee is excluded, because carriers do not refund it, and usage charges you have already incurred (texts sent and received, call minutes, AI usage) are deducted at cost, because our carriers and AI providers bill us for them. On 12/24-month plans the refund also deducts one month of service at the monthly rate, so the time you used is billed as if uncommitted.
pricing.faqCanadaFeeQ: What is the {canadaFeeMonthly}/mo Canadian messaging surcharge?
pricing.faqCanadaFeeA: Canadian mobile carriers (Bell, Rogers, Telus, and others) charge per-message fees for business texting that US carriers structure differently. Canadian-based businesses pay a flat {canadaFeeMonthly}/mo surcharge that covers these carrier pass-through fees, so your coworker can text your Canadian customers with no per-message surprises. It appears as its own line item at checkout and renews with your plan; US-based businesses never pay it.
pricing.faqKeepNumberQ: Can I keep my existing business number?
pricing.faqKeepNumberA: Yes. Standard and Enterprise plans support bring-your-own-number: we port your existing number in and your coworker answers on it. Every plan also includes a dedicated number out of the box.
pricing.faqExtraNumbersQ: Can I get more than one phone number?
pricing.faqExtraNumbersA: Every plan includes one dedicated number. Extra numbers are $5/mo each. Contact <email>{contactEmail}</email> to add one to your account.
pricing.faqUsageCapsQ: What happens if I use up my included voice minutes or SMS?
pricing.faqUsageCapsA: Your coworker stops placing metered voice calls or sending customer texts once the monthly cap is reached, and you get an alert well before that happens. Caps reset every month, and you can upgrade your plan at any time from the dashboard. You can also buy one-time voice minute or SMS top-up packs from Dashboard → Billing without upgrading — bonus usage is consumed after your included monthly allowance and lasts until the end of your billing period or 30 days from purchase, whichever is later.
pricing.faqWhiteGloveA: Two one-time packages: White-glove setup covers guided setup, number porting, and a live 1:1 training call; White-glove buildout adds everything in setup plus a full custom AiFlow buildout. Both include 30 days of priority call and video support. Tell us you're interested and a specialist will reach out.

faqPage.metaDescription: Frequently asked questions about New Coworker: how the AI employee works, setup, number porting, privacy, billing, usage limits, and support.
faqPage.ogDescription: How New Coworker works: setup, porting, privacy, billing, limits, and support.
faqPage.heroSubtitle: Everything owners ask before hiring their New Coworker. Something missing? Email us and a human replies.
faqPage.whatIsA: New Coworker is a 24/7 AI employee for your business. It answers your business phone with human-level conversation, replies to texts and emails, books appointments on your calendar, qualifies leads, and remembers every customer, all monitored from a dashboard you control.
faqPage.differentQ: How is this different from an answering service or a chatbot?
faqPage.differentA: An answering service takes messages; a chatbot follows scripts. Your coworker actually handles the interaction: it answers questions from your business's real knowledge, books appointments mid-call, sends follow-up texts and emails, and runs automated workflows. It also has permanent, lossless memory, so context builds instead of resetting every conversation.
faqPage.knowledgeQ: What does my coworker know about my business?
faqPage.knowledgeA: During onboarding you answer a short questionnaire, and your coworker can read your website to learn your services, pricing, and policies. From then on it learns from every conversation. You can review and edit its memory anytime from the dashboard.
faqPage.transfersQ: Can it transfer calls to me or my team?
faqPage.transfersA: Yes. Standard plans include warm handoff transfers: when a caller needs a human, your coworker brings you onto the line with the context already gathered, so the caller never repeats themselves.
faqPage.setupTimeA: Minutes. After checkout, provisioning is fully automated: your dedicated server, phone number, and email are created, and your coworker is trained from your questionnaire and website. No IT project, no sales call required.
faqPage.keepNumberQ: Can I keep my existing business number?
faqPage.keepNumberA: Yes. Standard and Enterprise plans support bring-your-own-number porting. We handle the port and your coworker answers on your existing number. Every plan also includes a dedicated number that works from day one.
faqPage.whiteGloveQ: Can someone set everything up for me?
faqPage.whiteGloveA: Yes. White-glove setup ($750) gets you a specialist who configures everything live with you, handles number porting, and trains you 1:1. White-glove buildout ($2,000) adds a full custom workflow buildout. Both include 30 days of priority call and video support. See pricing for details.
faqPage.whiteGlovePlain: Yes. White-glove setup ($750) gets you a specialist who configures everything live with you, handles number porting, and trains you 1:1. White-glove buildout ($2,000) adds a full custom workflow buildout. Both include 30 days of priority call and video support.
faqPage.carrierFeeQ: What is the one-time {carrierFee} carrier registration fee?
faqPage.carrierFeeA: US carriers require every business that sends text messages to complete 10DLC registration. We pass this one-time fee through at cost. It is charged at signup and is non-refundable because carriers do not refund it to us.
faqPage.dataA: Your AI coworker runs on a private server dedicated to your business, with its own credentials and a deny-by-default security posture. Conversations and business records are stored securely in our platform database, isolated per business and never shared with other businesses, and you can export your data at any time.
faqPage.trainingQ: Is my customer data used to train AI models?
faqPage.trainingA: No. Your coworker's memory belongs to your business and is used only to serve your customers. We follow a deny-by-default security posture with per-business credentials throughout the platform.
faqPage.complianceA: Compliance guardrails are enforced in every conversation, including Fair Housing Act rules for real estate, and Enterprise plans support custom compliance modules for other regulated industries.
faqPage.costA: Plans start at {starterPrice} on the 24-month Starter plan. Standard adds higher usage caps, {standardConcurrent}, Zapier, analytics, and more. Enterprise is custom and adds branded RCS messaging. Every plan has a 30-day money-back guarantee. See the full breakdown on the <link>pricing page</link>.
faqPage.costPlain: Plans start at {starterPrice} on the 24-month Starter plan. Standard adds higher usage caps, {standardConcurrent}, Zapier, analytics, and more. Enterprise is custom and adds branded RCS messaging. Every plan has a 30-day money-back guarantee.
faqPage.upfrontQ: Why is the full term billed upfront on 12 and 24-month plans?
faqPage.upfrontA: Because your plan includes a dedicated private server that we prepay for your whole contract. Billing the term once is what makes the discounted monthly rate possible. Monthly plans are billed month-to-month.
faqPage.cancelA: Yes. Every plan has a 30-day money-back window from the initial purchase date (the one-time carrier registration fee is excluded, usage charges already incurred — texts sent and received, call minutes, AI usage — are deducted at cost, and 12/24-month plans deduct one month of service at the monthly rate). After the term, service continues month-to-month at the renewal rate unless you start a new contract.
faqPage.capsQ: What happens if I hit my monthly usage caps?
faqPage.capsA: Included usage resets monthly: for example, Standard includes {standardVoice} and {standardSms} SMS per month. You're alerted before you run out; at the cap, metered voice calls and customer texts pause until the next cycle or an upgrade. You can also buy one-time voice minute or SMS top-up packs from Dashboard → Billing without upgrading — bonus usage is consumed after your included monthly allowance and lasts until the end of your billing period or 30 days from purchase, whichever is later. Compliance messages are never blocked.
faqPage.supportA: Starter includes email support; Standard includes priority email support; Enterprise includes an SLA with dedicated support. Live call/video support is available through the white-glove onboarding packages.
faqPage.ctaSubtitle: Every plan comes with a 30-day money-back guarantee.

about.metaDescription: New Coworker builds privacy-first digital employees for small businesses: autonomous AI coworkers that run on dedicated infrastructure and act like a real extension of your team.
about.ogDescription: Privacy-first digital employees for small businesses, on infrastructure dedicated to each customer.
about.heroSubtitle: New Coworker exists because the people who run small businesses are too busy running them to answer every call, chase every lead, and remember every detail. So we built an employee that does.
about.storyP1: The hardest part of a small business isn't the craft. It's the chaos around it. The call that comes in during a job. The lead that goes cold overnight. The follow-up that slips because the day ran long. Owners lose real revenue to work that is important but impossible to be present for.
about.storyP2: Big companies solve this with staff. We think small businesses deserve the same, without the payroll. New Coworker gives every business a digital employee that answers calls with human-level conversation, texts and emails customers, books appointments, qualifies leads, and follows up relentlessly, twenty-four hours a day.
about.storyP3: And because a coworker knows things a vendor never should, we made privacy the foundation: your business is strictly isolated from every other, business stays your business, and your coworker's memory works for you alone.

privacy.title: Privacy is architecture, not a policy page
privacy.description: Every business's AI coworker runs on its own dedicated server with its own credentials, and your data is isolated per business, never shared with other companies, and always yours to export. We built it this way because trust shouldn't require reading the fine print.

employee.title: An employee, not a chatbot
employee.description: Chatbots respond; coworkers act. Ours books appointments, sends follow-ups, updates records, transfers calls with context, and runs workflows, because small business owners need the work done, not another inbox to manage.

memory.title: Memory that compounds
memory.description: Typical AI tools forget everything between sessions. Your coworker keeps permanent, lossless memory of your business and customers, so every conversation makes the next one better.

stack.title: Own the stack, own the promise
stack.description: We run the infrastructure end to end (voice, messaging, memory, and automation), so when we promise your calls get answered, no third-party black box can break that promise.

zoomPage.metaDescription: How to connect Zoom to New Coworker, how your AI coworker creates Zoom meetings for booked appointments, and how to disconnect the integration.
zoomPage.ogDescription: Connect Zoom so your AI coworker schedules video appointments with Zoom join links — and keeps them updated when plans change.
zoomPage.heroSubtitle: Connect Zoom once and every video appointment your coworker books comes with a Zoom meeting and a join link the customer receives automatically — kept in sync through reschedules and cancellations.
zoomPage.card1Body: When a customer books a video appointment by phone, SMS, email, or web chat, your coworker creates a scheduled Zoom meeting on your account for that exact time slot.
zoomPage.card2Body: The meeting's join link rides along in the booking confirmation your customer receives and in the calendar event's description, so nobody hunts for the link.
zoomPage.card3Title: Kept in sync, end to end
zoomPage.card3Body: If the appointment is rescheduled, the Zoom meeting moves with it. If it's canceled, the meeting is deleted. No orphaned meetings on your account.
zoomPage.card4Body: Had a meeting you cloud-recorded with audio transcript on? Paste its meeting ID into Dashboard → Integrations → Zoom → Meeting minutes and your coworker fetches the transcript and condenses it into minutes in your document library — staff-only by default. Transcripts are only ever read when you ask for an import; nothing is pulled automatically.
zoomPage.prereqNote: Prerequisites: a New Coworker subscription with a connected calendar (Google, Microsoft 365, or CalDAV) and a Zoom account. Zoom meeting creation applies to appointments your coworker books directly on those calendars.
zoomPage.setupSubtitle: Connecting takes under a minute from your dashboard.
zoomPage.step1Body: Sign in at newcoworker.com, open <link>Dashboard → Integrations → Zoom</link>.
zoomPage.step2Title: Click Connect and approve on Zoom
zoomPage.step2Body: You're sent to Zoom's consent screen listing exactly what New Coworker may do (create, update, and delete meetings on your account). Click Allow. Zoom returns you to your dashboard automatically.
zoomPage.step3Body: The Zoom card now shows the connected account's name and email. From this moment, video appointments your coworker books include a Zoom meeting and join link.
zoomPage.usageSubtitle: There's nothing to operate day-to-day — your coworker does the work. Here's what happens behind the scenes.
zoomPage.booking1: A customer asks for an appointment (by phone, text, email, or web chat). Your coworker finds an open slot on your connected calendar and books it.
zoomPage.booking2: With Zoom connected, the booking gets a scheduled Zoom meeting at that time, titled after the appointment.
zoomPage.booking3: The customer's confirmation message includes the Zoom join link, and the link is written into the calendar event so your team sees it too.
zoomPage.changes1: When a customer reschedules, your coworker moves the calendar event and the Zoom meeting to the new time — same link, no new invite chains.
zoomPage.changes2: When a customer cancels, the meeting is deleted along with the event.
zoomPage.changes3: Zoom is never a point of failure: if Zoom is briefly unreachable, the appointment still books — it just books without a video link.
zoomPage.removalSubtitle: Two ways — both fully disconnect New Coworker from your Zoom account.
zoomPage.removeDash3: We revoke our access token with Zoom and permanently delete the stored credentials. Future bookings simply book without Zoom meetings.
zoomPage.removeZoom1: Sign in to marketplace.zoom.us and open Manage → Added Apps.
zoomPage.removeZoom3: Zoom revokes New Coworker's access immediately. Your dashboard card will show the connection as needing reconnection.
zoomPage.scopesTitle: Exactly what New Coworker can access
zoomPage.scopesSubtitle: The integration requests the minimum Zoom scopes needed to schedule meetings on your behalf and, only when you ask, import a meeting transcript — nothing else.
zoomPage.scopeUse1: Create the Zoom meeting for an appointment your coworker books
zoomPage.scopeUse2: Move the meeting when the appointment is rescheduled
zoomPage.scopeUse3: Delete the meeting when the appointment is canceled
zoomPage.scopeUse5: Create the join link your coworker sends to the customer
zoomPage.scopeUse6: Identify the connected Zoom account (name and email shown on your dashboard card)
zoomPage.scopeUse7: Fetch a cloud-recorded meeting's transcript — only when you click Import transcript — to produce meeting minutes in your Documents
zoomPage.privacyNote: Your Zoom tokens are encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM) in a row-level-security-protected database, are never exposed to the browser, and are deleted when you disconnect. Meeting content is read in exactly one case: when you explicitly import a cloud-recorded meeting's transcript for minutes — the transcript is processed into your document library and never pulled automatically. Everything else touches only the scheduling data for appointments your coworker manages. See our <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy> for details. Questions? Reach us any time via <contact>newcoworker.com/contact</contact>.

compareGhl.diffTitle: Tools wait for you. Employees don't.
compareGhl.themCardTitle: With GoHighLevel, a Meta lead means…
compareGhl.themCard1: The lead lands in your CRM pipeline.
compareGhl.themCard2: The workflows you built (and maintain) fire, if you built them.
compareGhl.themCard3: Replies come back to your unified inbox, waiting for a human.
compareGhl.themCard4: You (or staff you pay) work the conversation to a booking.
compareGhl.usCardTitle: With New Coworker, the same lead means…
compareGhl.usCard1: The lead is texted back within seconds, from your business number.
compareGhl.usCard2: Your coworker holds the conversation: answers questions, qualifies, books.
compareGhl.usCard3: The lead is filed with full context and routed to your team if you want.
compareGhl.usCard4: You get a summary. That's your whole job in the loop.
compareGhl.reviewsNote: Independent reviews consistently describe GoHighLevel as powerful but dense. Built for agencies, with a learning curve owners should plan weeks for. That's not a knock; it's a different product. If you want to run a marketing platform, buy the platform. If you want your phone answered and your leads worked, hire the coworker.
compareGhl.faqCheaperA: The $97 Starter plan is the entry ticket, not the running cost. To match what New Coworker does out of the box you'd add the AI Employee plan ($97/mo per location per HighLevel's published pricing), plus metered SMS, email, and voice usage. Plus your own hours configuring and maintaining it. New Coworker Standard is {standardMonthly} with the AI, the phone number, and generous usage quotas included, working on day one.
compareGhl.faqMetaQ: Can New Coworker capture my Facebook and Instagram ad leads like GoHighLevel?
compareGhl.faqMetaA: Yes. A guided in-dashboard setup connects your Meta lead ads in about 15 minutes, and from then on every lead is texted back within seconds, filed with full context, routed to your team if you want, and followed up automatically. The difference is what happens after capture: GoHighLevel stores the lead and runs the workflows you built; your coworker does this then handles the actual conversation too.
compareGhl.faqBetterQ: When is GoHighLevel the better choice?
compareGhl.faqBetterA: If you're an agency reselling software to clients, or you want to build funnels, websites, and courses inside one platform and have the time to run it, GoHighLevel is a strong product, that's what it was built for. New Coworker is built for the business owner who wants the phone answered and the leads worked without operating software.
compareGhl.faqBothA: Yes. Plenty of businesses keep their existing CRM and let New Coworker do the answering and follow-up. Zapier, webhooks, and our API push every call, text, and lead into whatever system you run including GoHighLevel.
compareGhl.faqSwitchA: Signup to live coworker takes minutes: pick a plan, answer a 15-minute interview, and your number, email, and trained coworker are provisioned automatically. You can port your existing business number in, import contacts by CSV, and there's a 30-day money-back guarantee.

compareGhl.ctaSubtitle: Live in minutes, Meta leads answered in seconds, 30-day money-back guarantee.

blogPage.title: Blog
blogPage.subtitle: Posts on newcoworker.com/blog — drafts, scheduling, and the weekly digest.
blogPage.empty: No posts yet — write the first one.
blogPage.digestEnabledHelp: Every Monday, if more than 10 pull requests merged that week, draft a plain-English feature roundup.
blogPage.digestAsDraftHelp: Off = the digest schedules itself to publish the same morning. On = it waits in drafts for review.
blogPage.digestIncludeImageHelp: Attach an AI-generated featured image to each digest post.
blogPage.igBusinessHelp: Business whose Marketing composer receives each published post (normally the HQ tenant). Leave empty to disable cross-posting.
blogPage.igImmediateHelp: Off = the cross-post lands as a composer draft for review. On = it goes straight to Instagram on publish.
blogPage.fieldSlugHelp: Auto-generated from the title when left empty.
blogPage.fieldExcerptHelp: Shown on the blog index and used as the Instagram caption — exactly what goes out.
blogPage.aiDraftPrompt: What should this post be about?
blogPage.deleteConfirm: Delete this post? This cannot be undone.

whiteGloveIntake.subject: Your NewCoworker white-glove setup questionnaire
whiteGloveIntake.line1: We're getting your white-glove build ready. To set everything up exactly the way you want it, we have a short questionnaire for you — mostly multiple choice, about 5 minutes.
whiteGloveIntake.line2: It covers how your AI assistant should greet new leads, when it follows up, how appointments get booked, and which topics should always go straight to your team.
whiteGloveIntake.line4: Your answers become the build plan our team installs from, so the more accurate they are, the better your assistant will fit your business from day one.
